video:video.video:title: Tides of War by Nalani
video:video.video:description: Tides of War: a soft, almost elegiac opening, the first traders on the horizon while the island still dances and Nalani reads the hunger behind their silk and lies. A cinematic short film from Mataala.

video:video.video:title: My Island School by Nalani
video:video.video:description: My Island School: a powerful story arc, a girl in a mission schoolroom learns the colonizer’s language and laws to use them back, rowing too far to ever be kept. A cinematic short film from Mataala.

video:video.video:title: Born For Storms by Nalani
video:video.video:description: Born For Storms: too loud, too sharp, made for war and trust, the storm that waits then burns the sky. A cinematic music video and short film from Mataala, a 19th-century Polynesian historical fiction.

video:video.video:title: Silent Watcher by Nalani
video:video.video:description: Silent Watcher: an overseer’s hand draws the maps and borders, an island marked for a quiet fate, until the jungle answers. A cinematic music video and short film from Mataala, an anti-colonial Polynesian historical fiction.

video:video.video:title: Blades of War by Nalani
video:video.video:description: Blades of War: a lone tribal drum opens hard and spaced, two forces meet on the sand, no kings, no names, just ash and flame, an ending that turns heavy and metal-tinged. A cinematic song from Mataala.

video:video.video:title: Mataala: A Short Film
video:video.video:description: Mataala, a short film: Turnbull arrives with ambitious plans for the island; Nalani, magnetic and articulate, refuses to be swayed. A 19th-century Polynesian historical fiction.
